description_long: This bonsai display features a small, round, earthen base with a central tree that has a slender, gently curving trunk and a sparse canopy of stylized, angular leaves. The foliage is dark with light highlights, giving a dramatic contrast against the muted brown of the base. Two small dinosaur figures are positioned on the ground, adding a prehistoric diorama effect to the scene. There are also two distinct clusters of angular, metallic-looking rocks or crystals, one larger and one smaller, placed on the base. The ground is textured to resemble soil, with a few scattered stones and patches of grass. The overall composition is both naturalistic and fantastical, blending organic and geometric elements.
description: A bonsai tree with a curving trunk and sparse, angular foliage stands on a round, earthen base, accompanied by dinosaur figures and metallic rock clusters. The scene combines natural and geometric features for a dramatic display.
description_view_0: The bonsai tree is central, with two dinosaur figures and a large, spiky metallic rock cluster visible on the right side.
description_view_1: The front view highlights the bonsai's trunk, both dinosaur figures, and the prominent metallic rock cluster in the foreground.
description_view_2: This angle shows the smaller metallic rock cluster and a single dinosaur, with the bonsai trunk and foliage in the background.
description_view_3: The bonsai tree and its trunk are central, with the smaller rock cluster and scattered stones more visible, and only one dinosaur in view.
attribute_pot_type: Round, shallow, earthen pot with a matte, unglazed finish and no visible decorative patterns.
attribute_trunk_curvature: The trunk is gently curved, following an informal upright style with a natural, flowing movement and a slight lean.
attribute_branch_arrangement: Branches are sparsely arranged, radiating outward from the trunk in an irregular, naturalistic pattern, with some angularity in their form.
attribute_leaf_density: Sparse, with clusters of leaves mainly at the branch tips, giving an open and airy appearance.
